definition: A title used for the Buddha, often carrying doctrinal weight that should not be flattened into a casual English equivalent.
notes: The project leaves Tathāgata untranslated because attempts to fully render it in English are unstable and usually distract from the doctrinal use of the title. A short gloss is acceptable on first occurrence when needed. The title often carries special doctrinal weight in statements about the Buddha, so the project avoids forcing a supposedly definitive English solution.

context_rules.context: In most source-facing and doctrinal contexts
context_rules.notes: Default project practice is to leave the title untranslated.

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose where a gloss is necessary
context_rules.notes: Use sparingly and only as a gloss.

context_rules.context: When the passage uses the title in doctrinally self-referential Buddha-speech
context_rules.notes: Keep the title explicit to preserve its technical and rhetorical force.

example_phrases.notes: Common titular usage throughout the Nikāyas.
Useful reminder that the title often appears in highly doctrinal compounds.
